title: RKLB Q1 Deep Dive: Space Systems Expansion, Neutron Progress, and Record Backlog Fuel Outlook
text: Rocket Lab announced Q1 2026 results exceeding expectations, with revenue of $200.3 million, representing 63.5% year-on-year growth and a 4.9% beat versus analyst estimates of $190.9 million. The company's adjusted earnings per share of -$0.02 surpassed consensus estimates of -$0.04. CEO Peter Beck highlighted the company's achievements, noting record backlog, record cash position and record launch contracts across multiple platforms. Q2 guidance projects $232.5 million in revenue at midpoint, exceeding analyst expectations by approximately 12%. Key performance drivers included robust demand for Electron and HASTE launches, completion of major contracts, and expansion through the Motive Space Systems acquisition. Management emphasized vertical integration efforts and progress on the Neutron medium-lift rocket program. Management noted that capital expenditures will remain elevated as we invest in testing, production scaling and infrastructure expansion heading toward Neutron's anticipated first flight later in 2026. The European market entry through the Mynaric acquisition closure was described as enabling new growth opportunities across EU, German, and UK government and commercial sectors.
